title: What Is True in This Present Moment
subtitle: A journal practice for grounding in what is actually happening right now
excerpt: A set of journal prompts for Day 8 of the sleep arc. The writing distinguishes between what is happening now and what the mind is projecting — anchoring the evening in present facts rather than future worries.
sourceNotes: Theme from Day 8 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795). Prompt structure original to homemade.education.

practice.whenToUse: On an evening when the mind keeps jumping to future problems or replaying past moments — use these sitting up before bed.

content.text: Journal prompts for Day 8 of the sleep arc. Use them before bed — sitting up, not lying down. The writing anchors the evening in what is actually true right now, not in what the mind is projecting.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
Where has my mind been running today?
Write freely — past conversations, future plans, worries. Get it on the page without judgement.
What is actually true in this present moment?
Name five present facts: what is in the room, what the body is doing, what is not happening right now. Plain sentences only.
Closer in
Which of today's worries are about something that exists right now, and which are projections?
Name each worry and write: present or projected? Don't judge the worries — just label them.
What can I do about any of this tonight?
Write what is actually actionable right now. Everything that isn't: write "tomorrow" next to it and mean it.
What is one true thing about right now that is fine?
Write one sentence about something that is actually okay in this specific moment. Small counts.
Where this practice comes from
Built from the Day 8 theme of
SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive
(Rebecca J Page, 2025): grounding in the present moment. Prompt structure original to homemade.education.
